_section: === A1 OBJECTIVES (from bosque_a1 blocks + CEFR matrix) ===

description: Introduce self, say name and where you are from
canDo: Can introduce themselves using simple phrases
gameAction: Naming Ritual: learner selects and pronounces basic identity phrases (yo soy, me llamo, soy de)
evidence: Correct word-sound associations; successful self-introduction in conversation game

description: Name objects with correct articles (el/la/los/las)
canDo: Can use simple words to identify objects
gameAction: Naming Ritual with visual support: learner matches nouns to images with correct articles
evidence: Accurate object identification; correct article selection across repeated encounters

description: Describe things, say where or whether they exist
canDo: Can give simple descriptions of immediate environment
gameAction: Guided description: learner fills in descriptions of the forest using ser/estar/hay + adjectives
evidence: Correct ser/estar distinction; appropriate adjective agreement

description: State age, count to 31, name days of the week, basic time of day
canDo: Can use numbers in everyday situations
gameAction: Counting and time: learner states age, identifies days, counts objects
evidence: Correct number usage; tener + años mastery

description: Express likes and dislikes, ask questions, negate, show possession
canDo: Can express simple preferences about familiar topics
gameAction: Preference expression: learner uses gustar/no gustar and possessives in dialogue
evidence: Correct gustar construction; appropriate negation; possessive accuracy

description: Describe daily routines using common -AR verbs
canDo: Can describe everyday activities
gameAction: Routine description: learner builds sentences about daily activities using -AR verbs
evidence: Correct -AR conjugation across persons; logical daily sequence

description: Order food, express physical needs (hunger, thirst, cold, heat)
canDo: Can handle simple transactions in shops and restaurants
gameAction: Food interaction: learner orders food and expresses needs in conversation
evidence: Correct tener + hambre/sed; appropriate food vocabulary

description: Introduce family members, ask who someone is
canDo: Can describe family members using simple phrases
gameAction: Family introduction: learner uses este/esta and quién to identify and introduce family
evidence: Correct demonstrative use; accurate family vocabulary

description: Describe home and rooms, express how you feel
canDo: Can describe their home and how they feel
gameAction: Home description: learner describes rooms and states using estar + adjective
evidence: Correct estar for states; accurate room vocabulary

description: State dates, tell time, count to 100
canDo: Can understand references to time and dates
gameAction: Time and date: learner identifies months, reads clocks, counts to 100
evidence: Correct month and time identification; number comprehension to 100

description: Use all three verb groups (-AR/-ER/-IR), say where you go, express reasons
canDo: Can use basic verb conjugation across three groups
gameAction: Verb integration: learner conjugates -AR/-ER/-IR verbs, uses ir + destination, por qué/porque
evidence: Correct conjugation across three groups; destination construction; causal expression

description: State nationality, describe in detail, integrate all A1 grammar
canDo: Can describe themselves and their background fully at A1 level
gameAction: Identity integration: learner combines all A1 skills to describe nationality, appearance, preferences
evidence: Integrated A1 output; nationality adjective agreement; fluent self-description

description: Recognize familiar words and very basic phrases related to immediate environment
canDo: Can recognize familiar words and very basic phrases concerning themselves, their family and immediate concrete surroundings
gameAction: Naming Ritual: learner selects and pronounces basic nouns (río, árbol, jaguar)
evidence: Correct word-sound associations; successful activation of environmental response

description: Understand simple instructions addressed carefully and slowly
canDo: Can understand and follow simple instructions addressed carefully and slowly
gameAction: Guided Movement: learner follows simple imperatives (cruza, gira)
evidence: Correct execution of movement actions; minimal need for repetition

description: Understand phrases related to areas of immediate relevance
canDo: Can understand sentences and frequently used expressions related to areas of most immediate relevance
gameAction: Expanded Naming Ritual with adjectives and basic phrases
evidence: Accurate selection and production of short noun phrases

description: Communicate in simple and routine tasks requiring a simple exchange of information
canDo: Can communicate in simple and routine tasks requiring a direct exchange of information on familiar topics
gameAction: Simple Story Weaving: assembling short sentences about daily actions
evidence: Successful sequencing of events; intelligible sentence construction

description: Give simple descriptions of background and immediate environment
canDo: Can describe in simple terms aspects of their background, immediate environment, and matters in areas of immediate need
gameAction: Guided Movement + Description: describing paths, places
evidence: Appropriate use of simple present and past tense; spatial language

description: Give negative commands in everyday situations
canDo: Can give simple instructions and commands using negative imperative
gameAction: Command practice: learner gives affirmative and negative commands in context
evidence: Correct imperative formation; appropriate no + subjunctive construction

description: Give warnings with interpersonal sensitivity using negative imperative
canDo: Can give simple warnings and reassurances using negative commands with interpersonal awareness
gameAction: Social warning: learner reassures a friend or receives an elder's warning using no + subjunctive
evidence: Correct negative imperative formation; appropriate interpersonal tone

description: Self-monitor and correct own language production
canDo: Can self-monitor and correct own production of previously learned forms
gameAction: Self-correction: learner evaluates and corrects own production in self-talk mode
evidence: Successful self-identification of errors; accurate self-correction of negative imperative forms

description: Describe daily routines in detail using reflexive verbs and expanded present tense
canDo: Can describe daily routines and habitual actions in some detail
gameAction: Routine narration: learner describes a full day using reflexive and non-reflexive verbs in expanded detail
evidence: Correct reflexive pronoun placement; coherent daily sequence; expanded vocabulary

description: Express needs, wants, and abilities using modal verbs + infinitive
canDo: Can express needs, wants, and abilities in simple terms
gameAction: Needs expression: learner uses modal + infinitive to communicate wants and needs in context
evidence: Correct modal conjugation; appropriate infinitive pairing; comparative usage

description: Describe weather, seasons, and environmental changes
canDo: Can describe weather conditions and seasonal changes using simple language
gameAction: Weather and change: learner describes weather conditions and connects them to feelings and states
evidence: Correct weather vocabulary; estar for changing states; emotional response to environment

description: Describe how things used to be, contrast habitual past with completed events
canDo: Can describe past habits and states, and distinguish them from completed actions
gameAction: Memory narration: learner describes the world as it used to be using imperfect, contrasts with what happened (preterite)
evidence: Correct imperfect formation; appropriate contrast between habitual and completed past

description: Integrate all A2 structures to describe people, narrate events, express needs, and state intentions
canDo: Can describe experiences, events, hopes and ambitions and briefly give reasons and explanations for opinions and plans
gameAction: Threshold narration: learner tells the full story — what was, what is, what will be — using all A2 structures in connected discourse
evidence: Integrated tense usage; coherent narrative across past/present/future; connected ideas with basic connectors

description: Narrate events and experiences using past tenses
canDo: Can narrate a story or describe something as a simple list of points, linking events in a broadly connected way
gameAction: Story Weaving: reconstructing and narrating past events
evidence: Coherent sequencing; appropriate use of preterite vs imperfect

description: Explain opinions or plans briefly, giving reasons
canDo: Can explain a viewpoint on a topical issue giving the advantages and disadvantages of various options
gameAction: Dialogue with Environment: explaining choices to a character or place
evidence: Logical explanation; comprehensible reasoning; basic subjunctive use

description: Deal with most situations likely to arise while traveling
canDo: Can deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling in an area where the language is spoken
gameAction: Guided Movement with contextual variation and narration
evidence: Successful adaptation of language to new but familiar situations

description: Retell stories heard from others, reporting what was said and maintaining cause-effect
canDo: Can report what others have said and relay simple stories in their own words
gameAction: Story retelling: learner listens to a character's story and retells it using indirect speech and narrative connectors
evidence: Accurate speech reporting; maintained cause-effect chain; appropriate tense backshifting

description: Understand that words can carry double meaning, irony, and connotation
canDo: Can understand implied meaning and recognize when language is used indirectly or ironically
gameAction: Meaning interpretation: learner identifies what a character really means versus what they literally say
evidence: Correct identification of implied meaning; recognition of ironic or indirect language use

description: Sustain extended narrative across multiple time frames with temporal framing
canDo: Can produce extended stretches of language with connected ideas, linking past, present, and habitual actions in sustained discourse
gameAction: Extended narration: learner tells a sustained story using temporal subordination, multiple time frames, and narrative connectors
evidence: Sustained discourse without breakdown; appropriate temporal framing; coherent multi-sentence narrative

description: Connect multiple narratives using relative clauses and cause-consequence structures
canDo: Can link separate accounts into a connected web, identifying how events in one story relate to events in another
gameAction: Narrative weaving: learner connects stories from different characters and places using relative clauses and causal connectors
evidence: Accurate relative clause formation; coherent cause-effect chains across narratives

description: Present arguments for and against a position using concessive structures
canDo: Can present clear arguments for and against a viewpoint, acknowledging opposing perspectives
gameAction: Argumentation: learner defends a position while acknowledging the opposing view using aunque, sin embargo, a pesar de
evidence: Coherent argumentation; correct concessive structure; subjunctive after value expressions

description: Express loss, irreversible change, and consequence using perfect tenses and passive voice
canDo: Can describe events that have consequences, express what has been lost or changed irreversibly
gameAction: Consequence narration: learner describes irreversible changes using present perfect and passive constructions
evidence: Correct present perfect formation; appropriate passive for impersonal loss; causal linking

description: Integrate all B1 structures to understand extended monologue, cultural register, and untranslatable concepts
canDo: Can understand extended speech and follow complex lines of argument on familiar topics, and recognize cultural and register variation
gameAction: Cultural listening: learner follows an elder's sustained speech, identifies register, and grapples with untranslatable concepts
evidence: Comprehension of extended monologue; recognition of cultural register; tolerance of untranslatable meaning

description: Interact with a degree of fluency and spontaneity
canDo: Can interact with a degree of fluency and spontaneity that makes regular interaction with native speakers quite possible
gameAction: Dialogue with the Living World: extended exchanges with branching outcomes
evidence: Sustained interaction without breakdown; appropriate turn-taking

description: Account for and sustain viewpoints with supporting arguments
canDo: Can present clear, detailed descriptions on a wide range of subjects related to their field of interest, expanding and supporting ideas with subsidiary points and relevant examples
gameAction: Comparative Story Weaving: defending a decision affecting the environment
evidence: Clear justification; use of connectors and evaluative language

description: Understand implicit meaning in complex interaction
canDo: Can understand the main ideas of complex text on both concrete and abstract topics
gameAction: Choice-based dialogue requiring pragmatic inference
evidence: Correct interpretation of indirect responses; appropriate follow-up actions

description: Hypothesize about abstract processes using si-clauses and metaphorical description
canDo: Can hypothesize about causes and consequences of abstract processes, and use metaphorical language to describe what cannot be seen directly
gameAction: Hypothesis building: learner describes an abstract threat using si-clauses and metaphor, connecting causes to invisible consequences
evidence: Correct si + imperfect subjunctive formation; coherent metaphorical description; abstract vocabulary use

description: Discuss fears, self-doubt, and identity using subjunctive for emotions and doubt
canDo: Can express and discuss fears, doubts, and internal conflicts using subjunctive structures triggered by emotional states
gameAction: Inner dialogue: learner confronts a mirror-self that speaks in doubt, responding with emotional subjunctive and conditional
evidence: Correct subjunctive after emotional triggers; appropriate conditional for hypothetical self-reflection; sustained inner monologue

description: Present sustained arguments using formal register and discourse markers
canDo: Can present a clear, well-structured argument using formal discourse markers and register-appropriate language
gameAction: Public speech: learner addresses a council using formal register, structured argumentation, and discourse markers
evidence: Appropriate formal register; coherent argument structure; correct discourse marker usage; sustained delivery

description: Interpret ambiguous information, analyze motives, and use conditional perfect for unrealized past
canDo: Can analyze conflicting accounts, identify what is uncertain, and hypothesize about what might have happened using conditional perfect
gameAction: Evidence analysis: learner compares conflicting testimonies, identifies contradictions, and hypothesizes using conditional perfect
evidence: Correct conditional perfect formation; accurate reported speech across accounts; identification of ambiguity without false resolution

description: Produce sustained creative expression using abstract and metaphorical language
canDo: Can produce extended creative description of abstract or metaphorical situations, naming what cannot be directly perceived
gameAction: Naming ritual (B2): learner speaks names back into existence in a metaphorical space, using abstract description and emotional expression
evidence: Sustained creative expression; coherent metaphorical language; abstract vocabulary deployed meaningfully

description: Layer complex information using relative clauses with prepositions and cuyo
canDo: Can produce complex descriptions using relative clauses with prepositions, cuyo/cuya, and distinguish indicative vs subjunctive in relatives to express known vs hypothetical referents
gameAction: River-branching descriptions: learner describes the landscape using nested relative clauses, each branch adding precision
evidence: Correct cuyo agreement; appropriate preposition+relative; indicative for known, subjunctive for hypothetical referents

description: Analyze literary texts identifying devices, tone, and authorial intent
canDo: Can identify literary devices (metaphor, simile, personification, hyperbole) in texts, analyze tone, and express interpretations using subjunctive for uncertainty
gameAction: Copla analysis: learner reads llanero poetry, identifies literary devices, and interprets authorial intent using hedged subjunctive
evidence: Correct identification of literary devices; appropriate tone analysis; subjunctive for interpretation (es posible que...)

description: Produce formal institutional discourse with hedging, nominalization, and data
canDo: Can produce formal written and spoken discourse using nominalization, hedging, impersonal constructions, and data-based argumentation appropriate to institutional contexts
gameAction: Regional report: learner presents economic data on Casanare using formal register, hedging, and impersonal constructions
evidence: Appropriate nominalization; correct hedging (podría considerarse, sería necesario); impersonal se constructions; data citation

description: Synthesize multiple perspectives into coherent argumentation with evidence evaluation
canDo: Can synthesize conflicting viewpoints from multiple sources, evaluate evidence quality, and draw balanced conclusions using concessive and synthesis structures
gameAction: Stakeholder tribunal: learner integrates biologist, rancher, and indigenous perspectives on biodiversity, evaluating evidence and drawing balanced conclusions
evidence: Accurate source reporting; appropriate concessive structures; evidence evaluation language; coherent synthesis

description: Integrate all B2 grammar into sustained discourse combining reflection, testimony, and formal debate
canDo: Can produce sustained discourse integrating pluperfect subjunctive, mixed conditionals, verbal periphrasis, passive voice, and argumentative synthesis in a coherent, purpose-driven text
gameAction: River tribunal: learner participates in a formal debate integrating personal reflection, community testimony, and structured argumentation — the capstone of B2
evidence: Correct pluperfect subjunctive; mixed conditional formation; sustained integration of multiple structures; coherent discourse

description: Express ideas fluently and spontaneously without obvious searching
canDo: Can express ideas fluently and spontaneously without much obvious searching for expressions
gameAction: Symbol Interpretation: verbalizing abstract meanings of symbols or visions
evidence: Fluid expression with minimal hesitation; accurate lexical choice

description: Use language flexibly for social, academic, or professional purposes
canDo: Can use language flexibly and effectively for social, academic and professional purposes
gameAction: Intention-driven dialogue adapting to audience and context
evidence: Register adaptation; stylistic appropriateness

description: Understand implicit and figurative meaning in complex texts
canDo: Can understand a wide range of demanding, longer texts, and recognise implicit meaning
gameAction: Metaphorical interpretation of mythic events
evidence: Accurate explanation of metaphor; contextual coherence

description: Express oneself spontaneously with precision and fine shades of meaning
canDo: Can express themselves spontaneously, very fluently and precisely, differentiating finer shades of meaning even in the most complex situations
gameAction: Authoring original mythic discourse affecting the world
evidence: Precise language choices; subtle differentiation of meaning

description: Mediate between speakers and viewpoints, resolving conflict through language
canDo: Can mediate effectively between speakers, handling sensitive situations with tact
gameAction: Restoring Balance: mediating conflicts between entities
evidence: Successful reformulation; resolution acceptable to multiple parties

description: Reformulate information effectively for different audiences and purposes
canDo: Can reformulate ideas in differing linguistic forms to convey emphasis, differentiate according to the situation, interlocutor, etc.
gameAction: Re-narrating events or arguments for different characters, communities, or contexts
evidence: Effective adaptation of discourse without loss of meaning; audience-appropriate register

description: Embed complex subordinate clauses with subjunctive for nuanced conditional, concessive, and purposive expression
canDo: Can use complex subordinate structures fluently to express conditions, concessions, purposes, and exceptions with appropriate subjunctive mood
gameAction: Complex narration: learner describes the mountain's voice using nested subordination and subjunctive
evidence: Correct subordinate clause embedding; appropriate subjunctive selection after conjunctions

description: Produce structured academic discourse with formal connectors, hedging, and nominalization
canDo: Can produce clear, well-structured academic text using formal register, hedging devices, and impersonal constructions
gameAction: Academic speech: learner structures a formal argument following the Mamo's teaching of precision and respect for complexity
evidence: Appropriate academic register; correct hedging; coherent argument structure

description: Navigate regional and social register variation across Colombian Spanish
canDo: Can recognize and adapt to regional dialectal variation, understanding that variation reflects lived experience rather than error
gameAction: Register navigation: learner identifies and adapts to paisa, costeño, bogotano, and llanero registers in dialogue
evidence: Correct register identification; appropriate code-switching; recognition of sociolinguistic variation

description: Use verbal periphrasis and idiomatic expressions fluently as markers of native-like competence
canDo: Can use periphrastic verbs and culturally embedded idiomatic expressions with natural fluency
gameAction: Weaving words: learner uses periphrastic and idiomatic expressions in context, understanding cultural metaphors embedded in language
evidence: Natural periphrastic verb use; culturally appropriate idiom deployment; fluent expression

description: Produce cohesive extended text using anaphora, cataphora, ellipsis, and thematic progression
canDo: Can produce clear, smoothly flowing, well-structured text with effective use of cohesive devices
gameAction: Thread weaving: learner connects ideas across paragraphs using cohesive devices, mirroring Kogi philosophy of invisible connections
evidence: Effective anaphoric reference; coherent thematic progression; smooth paragraph transitions

description: Analyze discourse for speech acts, presupposition, implicature, and power relations
canDo: Can analyze extended discourse to identify speech acts, detect presuppositions, interpret implicature, and recognize how language constructs power
gameAction: Temple reading: learner analyzes what is said, unsaid, and presupposed in the Antiguos' utterances
evidence: Correct speech act identification; accurate implicature interpretation; recognition of power dynamics in discourse

description: Construct and evaluate persuasive arguments using rhetorical devices and fallacy detection
canDo: Can construct sustained, persuasive arguments using appropriate rhetorical devices while identifying and avoiding logical fallacies
gameAction: Stone speech: learner defends conservation of Teyuna before the council using ethos, pathos, logos while detecting fallacies in opposition
evidence: Balanced rhetorical appeals; correct fallacy identification; sustained persuasive discourse

description: Identify and produce intertextual references, citations, and allusions in academic and literary contexts
canDo: Can identify intertextual references in complex texts and produce academic writing that integrates sources through citation, allusion, and paraphrase
gameAction: Palimpsest reading: learner identifies how texts speak through and with other texts, including historically silenced indigenous voices
evidence: Accurate citation; appropriate paraphrase; recognition of allusion; ethical source integration

description: Reflect on language as world-making, understanding that languages encode different ways of being
canDo: Can engage with philosophical questions about the relationship between language, thought, and reality using appropriate metalinguistic vocabulary
gameAction: Naming ritual: learner explores the Sapir-Whorf hypothesis through indigenous languages, understanding that language loss is the loss of entire ways of seeing
evidence: Metalinguistic vocabulary use; coherent philosophical argumentation; recognition of linguistic relativity

description: Integrate all C1 structures in sustained academic production with ethical communication
canDo: Can produce clear, well-structured complex text integrating subordination, academic register, rhetorical devices, and intertextuality in the service of ethical argumentation
gameAction: Star writing: learner produces an integrated academic essay defending Colombia's linguistic and ecological heritage
evidence: Sustained complex production; integrated C1 structures; ethical argumentation; academic rigor

description: Navigate all registers of Spanish with native-like sociolinguistic competence
canDo: Can switch fluidly between colloquial, formal, literary, and technical registers, understanding their social functions and transposing content across them
gameAction: Register transposition: learner rewrites the same content across four registers, demonstrating sociolinguistic mastery
evidence: Appropriate register selection; accurate transposition; awareness of social function of each register

description: Produce original literary texts using narrative voices, literary devices, and magical realism conventions
canDo: Can create original literary texts with conscious control of narrative voice, literary devices, and stylistic choices rooted in the Colombian literary tradition
gameAction: Literary workshop: learner creates original prose using controlled narrative voice, extended metaphor, and indirect free discourse
evidence: Conscious narrative voice choice; effective literary devices; stylistic coherence; original creative expression

description: Analyze language as an object using metalinguistic tools
canDo: Can identify and analyze types of ambiguity, speech acts, and pragmatic phenomena; can reflect on the limits and possibilities of language itself
gameAction: Mirror analysis: learner classifies ambiguity types, identifies speech acts, and reflects on linguistic relativity using Spanish metalanguage
evidence: Correct ambiguity classification; speech act identification; philosophical reflection on language using appropriate metalanguage

description: Produce and analyze public discourse using rhetorical strategies
canDo: Can compose and deliver persuasive public discourse using ethos/pathos/logos, oratory resources, and audience-adapted register
gameAction: Public speech: learner addresses an audience using rhetorical strategies, adapting persuasion to context and audience
evidence: Effective rhetorical strategy deployment; audience adaptation; coherent thesis with oratory resources

description: Synthesize register, literary, metalinguistic, and rhetorical competence into unified discourse
canDo: Can integrate all C2 Basic competences — register mastery, literary creation, metalinguistic analysis, and public rhetoric — into sustained, purposeful discourse about language and culture
gameAction: Observatory synthesis: learner integrates all C2 Basic domains to argue that language learning is an act of cultural care
evidence: Multi-domain integration; sustained purposeful discourse; philosophical depth with linguistic precision

_section: === C2 Advanced — La palabra del corazón (dest54–dest58) ===

description: Mediate between languages, cultures, and worldviews through translation and reformulation
canDo: Can mediate effectively between languages and cultural worldviews, understanding that translation involves creative cultural adaptation, not mere word substitution
gameAction: Cultural bridge: learner mediates between indigenous and scientific worldviews, reformulating concepts across registers and languages
evidence: Effective mediation; cultural sensitivity; recognition of untranslatable concepts; register-appropriate reformulation

description: Produce professional texts with precision, revision discipline, and genre awareness
canDo: Can produce professional texts (chronicle, essay, report, opinion column) with conscious revision, precise verb choice, and genre-appropriate structure
gameAction: Writing workshop: learner drafts, revises, and refines a professional text demonstrating genre awareness and precision
evidence: Genre-appropriate structure; precise language; effective revision; connector mastery; stylistic coherence

description: Analyze linguistic variation, attitudes, and language endangerment with sociolinguistic tools
canDo: Can analyze diatopic, diastratic, diaphasic, and diachronic variation; can identify language attitudes and their social consequences; can discuss language endangerment with analytical rigor
gameAction: Variation analysis: learner maps Colombian linguistic diversity, identifying prestige dynamics and endangerment patterns
evidence: Correct variation classification; language attitude analysis; understanding of endangerment mechanisms

description: Transmit cultural and linguistic knowledge to others using pedagogical discourse
canDo: Can explain complex linguistic and cultural concepts simply without falsification, using analogy, scaffolding, and narrative as pedagogical tools
gameAction: Teaching circle: learner explains a complex grammar concept to different audiences using analogy, narrative, and scaffolded simplification
evidence: Accurate simplification without falsification; effective analogy; audience-adapted explanation

description: Demonstrate total mastery — the spirit of the jaguar
canDo: Can deploy all six linguistic domains simultaneously, oscillate registers within single discourse, and use language as an instrument of care, mediation, and creation — La Palabra del Corazón
gameAction: The final chamber: learner produces discourse integrating all domains, all registers, all skills — and discovers that the Heart Word is escuchar
evidence: Total domain integration; register oscillation; metalinguistic awareness; autonomous creative production; the answer is escuchar
